context_blocks.title: Runtime context 1
context_blocks.text: ok so i have all the cli worker

runtime_understanding.runtime_summary: This repository provides a developer-oriented documentation generator with a Streamlit UI frontend (ui/streamlit_app.py) that calls a FastAPI-based backend (app.*) to: scan a local Python project, save scans, compare them, generate LLM-powered structured understandings, build HTML documentation, and save/serve the generated docs. The Streamlit UI can attach ordered runtime/context blocks and screenshots which are uploaded to the backend and optionally analyzed with a vision-capable LLM to enrich the final documentation. The screenshot shows the developer running the Streamlit UI locally (port 8501).

tooling_stack.purpose: Interactive frontend for developers to trigger scans, attach runtime/context blocks and screenshots, view document history and open generated HTML docs.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py functions such as sync_documentation_with_context, build_context_blocks_payload, normalize_api_url; screenshot shows command `streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py` and Local URL http://localhost:8501

tooling_stack.purpose: Backend HTTP API exposing endpoints to scan projects, compare scans, generate understandings, build and serve HTML documentation, and accept runtime/context uploads.
tooling_stack.evidence: Many API routes listed in project scan: /api/projects/documentation/sync, /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context, /api/projects/scan, /api/projects/{project_name}/documents etc. (module app.api.project_routes)

tooling_stack.purpose: Persist generated HTML, document metadata and copy runtime assets (screenshots) into per-project asset directories.
tooling_stack.evidence: DocumentStorage class docstring and calls in _generate_and_save_document and sync_project_documentation_with_context that use document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets and document_storage.save_document

tooling_stack.tool: Project scanner / parser / analyzer (app.services.project_scanner, app.services.code_parser, app.services.project_analyzer)
tooling_stack.purpose: Scan file tree, parse Python source into developer-reference data (modules, routes, functions, classes, signatures) and compute project statistics used as the canonical reference in docs generation.
tooling_stack.evidence: ProjectScanner, PythonCodeParser and ProjectAnalyzer classes and docstrings; called from sync endpoints (project_scanner.scan_project)

tooling_stack.purpose: Generate narrative structured project 'understanding' and optionally analyze runtime screenshots using a configured LLM (OpenAI / Anthropic / Claude).
tooling_stack.evidence: get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model in app.core.config; provider implementations in app.services.llm.openai_provider and anthropic_provider; functions _generate_and_save_understanding and _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm reference LLM usage

tooling_stack.purpose: Save uploaded screenshots to temporary files, copy them into document storage, and clean temp files after processing.
tooling_stack.evidence: Functions _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp and _cleanup_temp_files in app.api.project_routes; ui.build_context_blocks_payload builds multipart payload

tooling_stack.purpose: Streamlit frontend uses requests to call the backend endpoints and to fetch document history / open documents.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui.streamlit_app functions use requests.post and requests.get (sync_documentation, sync_documentation_with_context, get_document_history)

runtime_flow.title: Start the Streamlit UI
runtime_flow.description: Run the Streamlit frontend locally which launches a developer UI (default shown on port 8501). The UI lets the user provide a project path, optional human-written additional_context and ordered runtime context blocks, and screenshots.
runtime_flow.evidence: Screenshot terminal contains command `streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py` and shows Streamlit/UVicorn server started on 0.0.0.0:8501 with Local URL http://localhost:8501; ui/streamlit_app.py defines UI helper functions (normalize_api_url, build_context_blocks_payload, sync_documentation_with_context).

runtime_flow.title: User provides project path and runtime context
runtime_flow.description: From the UI the user supplies the absolute project path and can optionally add additional context, an ordered list of runtime/context blocks (title, text, screenshot_index), and paste/upload screenshots. The UI builds a multipart payload: JSON context_blocks_json + attached screenshot files.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync_project_documentation_with_context endpoint signature accepts Form fields and files; ui.build_context_blocks_payload assembles context_blocks_json and files; user metadata shows one screenshot and context_blocks with screenshot_index mapping.

runtime_flow.title: Frontend POSTs to backend sync-with-context endpoint
runtime_flow.description: The UI posts the form to /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context (or /documentation/sync) with the project path, context_blocks_json and screenshots. Backend saves the scan and any uploaded assets, then builds runtime_context by associating blocks with uploaded images.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes.sync_project_documentation_with_context called functions include project_scanner.scan_project, scan_storage.save_scan, _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp, document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets, _build_runtime_context_from_blocks, and document_storage.list_documents

runtime_flow.title: Scan saved and compared, create or update understanding
runtime_flow.description: Backend saves the new scan. If a previous document exists it retrieves the older scan and runs scan_comparator.compare_scans to determine whether meaningful doc-relevant changes exist. If changes exist (or this is initial), the backend requests the configured LLM to generate a structured project understanding and persists it.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync_project_documentation/_generate_and_save_understanding use project_scanner, scan_storage, scan_comparator.compare_scans, project_understanding_service.generate_understanding, and understanding_storage.save_understanding

runtime_flow.title: Optional runtime context analysis with vision-capable LLM
runtime_flow.description: If runtime context blocks or screenshots were provided, the backend may call runtime_context_analyzer.analyze to let a vision-capable LLM interpret screenshots together with code scan context and user notes, producing analyzed runtime_context that is included in generated docs.
runtime_flow.evidence: _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm calls get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model and runtime_context_analyzer.analyze; sync_project_documentation_with_context passes runtime_context into _generate_and_save_document

runtime_flow.title: HTML document built and stored; results returned
runtime_flow.description: DocumentBuilder creates the final standalone HTML from the stored understanding (and runtime_context if present). DocumentStorage saves the HTML and runtime assets under the workspace documents directory. API returns a DocumentationSyncResponse describing action (created/updated/unchanged) and metadata including document_id and URLs.
runtime_flow.evidence: _generate_and_save_document calls document_builder.build_html and document_storage.save_document; generate_project_document endpoint shows saving and returning DocumentGenerationResponse

runtime_flow.title: Developer opens or downloads HTML doc
runtime_flow.description: The front-end or developer can open the generated HTML via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html which returns a FileResponse for the saved HTML file.
runtime_flow.evidence: API route get_project_document_html returns FileResponse using document_storage.get_document_file; ui.streamlit_app.build_document_url constructs the access URL

screenshot_insights.title: Streamlit UI launched locally
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal running `streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py`, server started and exposing Local URL http://localhost:8501 and network URL http://192.168.43.184:8501.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms how the developer starts the frontend and which port the interactive UI is reachable on. This is the entrypoint for adding runtime context and screenshots that are sent to the backend.

screenshot_insights.title: User workflow: attaching runtime screenshot(s)
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: User-provided screenshot (context_block_001.png) is present in uploaded metadata and UI context_blocks includes a block referencing that screenshot.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Verifies the project supports screenshot upload + mapping to ordered context blocks (screenshot_index). The backend functions _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp, document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets and _build_runtime_context_from_blocks implement this mapping and lifecycle.

operational_notes: Start the Streamlit frontend: `streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py` (screenshot confirms server at port 8501).
The Streamlit UI will POST either JSON-only sync (documentation/sync) or multipart sync-with-context (documentation/sync-with-context) including context_blocks_json and screenshots.

Workspace directories must exist or will be created by create_required_directories (app.core.config). Generated data is saved under workspace/scans, workspace/understandings and workspace/documents as described in storage docstrings.

LLM integration requires environment variables configured per app.core.config (LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific api keys/models). Missing or invalid values will raise errors (get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model).

Uploaded screenshots are saved to temporary files then copied into document storage and cleaned up by _cleanup_temp_files; ensure the process has filesystem permissions to create temp files and write to workspace directories.
Streamlit UI client functions (sync_documentation_with_context) use requests with timeouts; when backend is remote, set normalize_api_url to the backend base URL in the UI.

risks_or_gaps.title: Missing or misconfigured LLM credentials
risks_or_gaps.description: LLM provider, API key or model must be set in environment variables. If not configured correctly, attempts to generate project understanding or analyze screenshots will fail and endpoints will raise HTTP 4xx/5xx errors.

risks_or_gaps.title: Screenshot-index mapping errors
risks_or_gaps.description: Runtime context blocks rely on screenshot_index integers to map uploaded files to context blocks. If the UI payload's indices and uploaded file order are inconsistent, blocks may receive wrong or no images leading to inaccurate runtime analysis.

risks_or_gaps.title: Temporary file lifecycle and permissions
risks_or_gaps.description: Uploaded screenshots are written to temporary files before being copied into document storage. If temp files cannot be created or removed (permissions, antivirus, or cleanup failures), disk buildup or upload errors can occur.

risks_or_gaps.title: Potential cost and hallucination from LLM-based analysis
risks_or_gaps.description: The runtime_context_analyzer uses a vision-capable LLM to interpret screenshots and generate narrative context. This can incur API costs and may produce incorrect interpretations; the system partially mitigates hallucination by merging machine-produced narrative with parser/analyzer factual outputs, but risk remains.

risks_or_gaps.title: No running backend server visible in screenshot
risks_or_gaps.description: The screenshot shows the Streamlit UI server on port 8501 but does not show a separately running FastAPI backend. If the backend is expected to run separately, developers must run the FastAPI server (uvicorn) or ensure the UI is pointed to a reachable backend URL.
